title: EU PRIIPs Key Information Document Template
content:
This Key Information Document (KID) template has been developed by Meridian Wealth Partners to ensure full compliance with the Packaged Retail and Insurance-based Investment Products (PRIIPs) Regulation (EU) 1286/2014. All advisors and senior personnel must utilize this standardized framework when preparing client documentation for EU-regulated investment products.

The PRIIPs regulation mandates that all packaged retail investment products offered to retail investors within the European Union must be accompanied by a standardized three-page KID. This document must present essential information in a clear, understandable format, enabling clients to compare different investment products effectively. Meridian Wealth Partners has identified approximately 347 investment vehicles within our current product portfolio that fall under PRIIPs scope, requiring individual KID preparation.

Key sections that must be completed for each product include: What is this product and what are the risks? What are the costs? How long should I hold it and can I take money out early? How can I complain? Other relevant information. The risk indicator must display a numerical scale from 1 (lower risk) to 7 (higher risk), calculated using the methodology prescribed in Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2017/653.

Cost disclosure requirements under PRIIPs are particularly stringent. Total cost ratios must be expressed as reduction in yield (RIY) over recommended holding periods of 1, 3, and 5 years where applicable. For structured products, performance scenarios must include stress, unfavorable, moderate, and favorable outcomes based on historical data spanning the previous 5 years or, where insufficient data exists, appropriate proxy benchmarks.

Meridian Wealth Partners maintains a centralized PRIIPs database containing pre-approved KIDs for all compliant products. Advisors must verify that any KID accessed from this system displays a review date no older than 12 months. Where material changes occur to underlying product terms, costs, or risk profiles, updated KIDs must be prepared within 30 business days.

Specific attention must be paid to currency risk disclosure for non-EUR denominated products. Where clients are investing in USD, GBP, CHF, or other non-EUR instruments, the KID must clearly state: "You may receive back less than you invest due to currency fluctuations." Historical currency volatility data must inform the overall risk classification.

For insurance-based investment products (IBIPs), additional disclosures regarding insurance coverage, surrender values, and policy terms are mandatory. Meridian's IBIP portfolio, representing approximately 23% of our EU product shelf, requires enhanced due diligence processes including annual solvency ratio verification for underlying insurance providers.

Digital delivery of KIDs is permitted under PRIIPs regulation, provided clients explicitly consent to electronic communication. Our client portal automatically logs KID acknowledgment timestamps and maintains audit trails for regulatory examination purposes. Paper delivery remains available upon request at no additional charge.

Compliance monitoring includes quarterly reviews of all active KIDs, cost accuracy verification through independent fund data providers, and annual third-party validation of risk classification methodologies. Any advisor-level concerns regarding KID accuracy or completeness should be escalated immediately to the Regulatory Affairs team via our internal compliance system.
